line: <!-- Grammar-driven MediaWiki fixture; all constructs are deliberately closed. -->
'''Expedition Aurora''' is a fictional research voyage from [[Earth]] to [[Mars]].
The crew carries instruments labeled λ, 東京, 🚀, and 𝌆 for Unicode coverage.
See [https://example.org/aurora mission control] and RFC 9110 for background.
The plan combines '''robotic scouting''' with ''human fieldwork''.
An optional argument appears as {{{motto|Per aspera ad astra}}}.
The localized title is -{zh-hans:曙光任务;zh-hant:曙光任務}-.
The archive identifier is ISBN 978-1-4028-9462-6 and the study is PMID 123456.
Each profile uses a small nested template: {{Badge|role={{Role|science}}|color=blue}}.
The callsign is <nowiki>[[Aurora-1]] {{literal}}</nowiki>, not wiki markup.

line: # Seal the tube and attach a barcode.
# Place it in the return canister.
<ref name="protocol">Open Space Institute, ''Sample Handling Manual'', revision 7.</ref>
<ref name="unicode">Labels include BMP text 日本語 and astral text 🚀 𝌆.</ref>
The handling protocol is independently reviewed.<ref name="protocol" />
Mission control publishes a daily bulletin at [https://status.example.org/aurora status.example.org].
Messages use {{lang|fr|« Bon voyage »}} and {{lang|ja|安全第一}} where appropriate.
After the closing tag, [[Main Page|normal parsing]] resumes.
This section documents a hypothetical transclusion.
The extension comment is closed before this sentence.
